title: hakea lorea subsp. lorea
intro: Hakea lorea subsp. lorea, also known as the Tasmanian hakea, is a species of shrub native to Tasmania and southeastern Australia, characterized by its prickly leaves and clusters of white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Hakea lorea subsp. lorea include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, and can also be found in some commercial cough medicines and expectorants.
